headline: How The Gogo (GOGO) Story Is Shifting With Softer 2026 Outlook And Capital Return Hopes
summary: The headline change for Gogo is a cut in the price target from US$11 to US$7, even as a separate model fair value figure remains at US$9.33 per share. That shift reflects analyst concerns around softer 2026 guidance and pressure on legacy revenue, balanced against improving leverage and the potential for capital returns. As you read on, you will see how these moving parts shape the current analyst narrative and what to watch as it continues to evolve. Analyst Price Targets don't always...

headline: Have Other Epstein Crimes Gone Unpunished?
summary: In one sense, the Jeffrey Epstein saga has been a massive prosecutorial opportunity lost.  The same can be said of potential financial malfeasance committed by individuals named in the Epstein files—with one glaring difference.  Coming up on seven years after Epstein’s death, investigators can still redeem themselves in the case of sex crimes and look to prosecute individuals, as in many instances there is no statute of limitations for sex trafficking and rape.

content.title: Have Other Epstein Crimes Gone Unpunished?
content.summary: In one sense, the Jeffrey Epstein saga has been a massive prosecutorial opportunity lost.  The same can be said of potential financial malfeasance committed by individuals named in the Epstein files—with one glaring difference.  Coming up on seven years after Epstein’s death, investigators can still redeem themselves in the case of sex crimes and look to prosecute individuals, as in many instances there is no statute of limitations for sex trafficking and rape.

headline: Is the Stock Market Open Today? Here Are the Trading Hours for Easter Monday.
summary: Here’s what to know about trading hours for Easter Monday.  The New York Stock Exchange and the Nasdaq Stock Market will open at their regular time, 9:30 a.m. Eastern.  Similarly, over-the-counter markets, the Cboe Futures Exchange, the CME Globex trading platform, and the bond market will be open at their regular hours.

content.title: Is the Stock Market Open Today? Here Are the Trading Hours for Easter Monday.
content.summary: Here’s what to know about trading hours for Easter Monday.  The New York Stock Exchange and the Nasdaq Stock Market will open at their regular time, 9:30 a.m. Eastern.  Similarly, over-the-counter markets, the Cboe Futures Exchange, the CME Globex trading platform, and the bond market will be open at their regular hours.

headline: Jamie Dimon Says Inflation Could Be 'Skunk at the Party'
summary: The JPMorgan Chase CEO warned that the war in Iran could push up inflation and drag down financial markets even further if interest rates start to rise.  In his annual letter to shareholders, Dimon said there is a risk of more oil and commodity price shocks in the months ahead, which could lead to prolonged inflation and ultimately higher interest rates.  “The skunk at the party—and it could happen in 2026—would be inflation slowly going up,” Dimon wrote.

headline: Dimon: Global Economy 'Far Less Reliant' On Energy Than In The Past
summary: Risks related to the ongoing war in Ukraine and newly erupted conflict in Iran have the potential to spiral out of control, JPMorgan Chase's CEO wrote.  Since the war started on Feb. 28, Iran has stopped ships linked to the U.S., Israel and their allies from passing through the Strait of Hormuz, the shipping route for oil, natural gas and other exports out of the Persian Gulf.

headline: This Is How Much Surging Oil Prices Will Boost Inflation
summary: More economists are hiking inflation forecasts and dashing expectations for interest-rate cuts this year as the spike in crude oil prices dents U.S. consumers' purchasing power.  In its latest economic outlook, Morningstar expects inflation to jump to 3.3% this year, up from 2.6% in 2025, mainly because of a 0.5-percentage-point boost from energy prices.  It estimates higher crude oil prices will contribute about 0.65 percentage points to personal consumption expenditures (PCE), another inflation gauge, in 2026.

content.title: This Is How Much Surging Oil Prices Will Boost Inflation
content.summary: More economists are hiking inflation forecasts and dashing expectations for interest-rate cuts this year as the spike in crude oil prices dents U.S. consumers' purchasing power.  In its latest economic outlook, Morningstar expects inflation to jump to 3.3% this year, up from 2.6% in 2025, mainly because of a 0.5-percentage-point boost from energy prices.  It estimates higher crude oil prices will contribute about 0.65 percentage points to personal consumption expenditures (PCE), another inflation gauge, in 2026.

headline: JPMorgan's Dimon signals PE firms likely to struggle in a bear market
summary: JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon Kevin Dietsch/Getty Images Jamie Dimon has flagged private equity’s current predicament—holding periods nearly doubling, IPOs underwhelming and continuation funds proliferating—and posed an uncomfortable question: What happens when the good times end? headline: Goldman Says It’s Ready to Pounce as Retail Flees Private Credit
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Goldman Sachs Group Inc. says a reliance on stickier, more patient institutional investors, rather than wealthy individuals, helped its $15.7 billion private credit fund narrowly escape the broader exodus plaguing peers this year.Now, it’s looking to capitalize on the retreat of retail money to swoop in while rivals pull back.Goldman Sachs Private Credit Corp., which manages a so-called non-traded business development company, met redemption requests in the first quarter amounting
